t1_fidelity_table.upstream_quote: Surrogate primary key, auto-incremented. NOT FOR REPLICATION indicates replication topology. Uniquely identifies each split event.
t1_fidelity_table.wiki_quote: Sequential integer primary key for the split ratio record. Passed through from PriceLog.History.SplitRatio without transformation.
t1_fidelity_table.loss: Dropped IDENTITY, NOT FOR REPLICATION detail, 'split event' specificity; completely reworded

t1_fidelity_table.upstream_quote: The stock instrument this split applies to. FK to Trade.Instrument. CHECK constraint enforces InstrumentID > 1000 - only stock instruments (not forex or crypto).
t1_fidelity_table.wiki_quote: Instrument identifier (FK to DWH_dbo.Dim_Currency.CurrencyID and DWH_dbo.Dim_Instrument.InstrumentID). Groups all split ratio records for a single tradeable instrument.
t1_fidelity_table.loss: Dropped CHECK >1000 constraint, stocks-only restriction, changed FK target from Trade.Instrument to Dim_Currency

t1_fidelity_table.upstream_quote: Start of the period this split ratio is effective. Default '2000-01-01' means 'from the beginning of the instrument's history.' The split adjustment applies to transactions from this date forward until MaxDate.
t1_fidelity_table.wiki_quote: Start of the date range (inclusive) for which the ratio applies. `2000-01-01` is the beginning-of-history sentinel for the earliest period before any splits.
t1_fidelity_table.loss: Reworded; dropped 'applies to transactions from this date forward until MaxDate'

t1_fidelity_table.upstream_quote: End of the period this split ratio is effective (exclusive). Sentinel value '2100-01-01' means 'currently active - no end date set.' When a new split occurs, the current active row's MaxDate is set to the new split's MinDate.
t1_fidelity_table.wiki_quote: End of the date range (exclusive) for which the ratio applies. `2100-01-01` is the open-ended sentinel indicating the currently active ratio (no further splits yet).
t1_fidelity_table.loss: Dropped update behavior ('current active row's MaxDate is set to the new split's MinDate')

t1_fidelity_table.upstream_quote: Multiplier applied to historical prices after this split. Equal to UnitsBefore/UnitsAfter. For a 2-for-1 split: PriceRatio=0.5 (price halved). For a 1-for-2 reverse split: PriceRatio=2. CHECK constraint enforces > 0. Default 1 = no adjustment.
t1_fidelity_table.wiki_quote: Cumulative price adjustment multiplier for this period. Multiply a historical price by this value to get its split-adjusted equivalent. 1.0 means no adjustment. Example: PriceRatio=0.25 means a 4:1 stock split occurred (1 old share = 4 new shares, price adjusted down to 25%).
t1_fidelity_table.loss: Dropped UnitsBefore/UnitsAfter formula, CHECK >0 constraint, changed examples, added 'cumulative' not in upstream

t1_fidelity_table.upstream_quote: Multiplier applied to position unit counts after this split. Equal to UnitsAfter/UnitsBefore. For a 2-for-1 split: AmountRatio=2 (units doubled). For a 1-for-2 reverse split: AmountRatio=0.5. CHECK constraint enforces > 0. Default 1 = no adjustment.
t1_fidelity_table.wiki_quote: Cumulative amount/quantity adjustment multiplier for this period. Multiply a historical position size by this value to get the split-adjusted share count. Inverse of PriceRatio: AmountRatio=4.0 corresponds to PriceRatio=0.25 (4:1 split).
t1_fidelity_table.loss: Dropped formula (UnitsAfter/UnitsBefore), CHECK constraint, changed examples, added 'cumulative'

t1_fidelity_table.upstream_quote: Original unadjusted price ratio stored as money type. Before cumulative split adjustments are applied. Used for audit and comparison.
t1_fidelity_table.wiki_quote: Incremental (non-cumulative) price ratio from the most recent split event only, before stacking with prior splits. Used to isolate the effect of a single split. 1.0 for the oldest period (before any splits).
t1_fidelity_table.loss: Dropped 'money type', fabricated 'incremental/non-cumulative' semantics and 'most recent split event only' not stated in upstream

t1_fidelity_table.upstream_quote: Original unadjusted amount ratio stored as money type. Before cumulative adjustments.
t1_fidelity_table.wiki_quote: Incremental (non-cumulative) amount ratio from the most recent split event only. Inverse of PriceRatioUnAdjusted for the current split. 1.0 for the oldest period (before any splits).
t1_fidelity_table.loss: Dropped 'money type', fabricated 'incremental' semantics not in upstream

issues.column_or_section: ID, InstrumentID, MinDate, MaxDate, PriceRatio, AmountRatio, PriceRatioUnAdjusted, AmountRatioUnAdjusted
issues.problem: All 8 passthrough columns from PriceLog.History.SplitRatio are mistagged as Tier 2 or Tier 3. Upstream wiki (History.SplitRatio.md) was available in the bundle. All should be Tier 1 with verbatim upstream descriptions.
Dropped CHECK constraint (InstrumentID > 1000 — stocks only, not forex or crypto) and changed FK target from Trade.Instrument to Dim_Currency. Critical business rule lost.

Upstream says 'Original unadjusted price ratio stored as money type. Before cumulative split adjustments are applied. Used for audit and comparison.' Writer fabricated 'Incremental (non-cumulative) price ratio from the most recent split event only' — semantics not in upstream wiki.
Dropped computation formulas (UnitsBefore/UnitsAfter, UnitsAfter/UnitsBefore) and CHECK >0 constraints from upstream. Added 'cumulative' qualifier not present in upstream text.
Footer claims '0 T1, 3 T2, 6 T3' when 8 columns should be T1 and only 1 (UpdateDate) should be T2. Signals upstream bundle was never consulted.

verdict.regeneration_feedback: Re-run with: (1) Re-tag all 8 passthrough columns (ID, InstrumentID, MinDate, MaxDate, PriceRatio, AmountRatio, PriceRatioUnAdjusted, AmountRatioUnAdjusted) as Tier 1 — History.SplitRatio and use VERBATIM descriptions from the upstream wiki at DB_Schema/etoro/Wiki/History/Tables/History.SplitRatio.md. (2) For InstrumentID, preserve the CHECK >1000 constraint and FK to Trade.Instrument from upstream. (3) For PriceRatioUnAdjusted and AmountRatioUnAdjusted, use upstream text verbatim — do NOT fabricate 'incremental' semantics. (4) For PriceRatio and AmountRatio, include UnitsBefore/UnitsAfter formulas and CHECK >0 constraint from upstream. (5) Add explicit data date-range to Section 1. (6) Update footer tier counts to 8 T1, 1 T2.

skipped_phases: Phases 9/14 — specific skipped phases not enumerated in footer
